title: Kasambahay (domestic workers)
rationale: High informal employment and deeply personal nature of domestic work create significant barriers to AI/robotic replacement. Philippine households prefer human relationships and affordable manual labor over expensive technological solutions.

title: Barangay health workers
rationale: High informal sector presence and deeply personal community health interactions create significant barriers to AI replacement. Community trust and interpersonal communication remain critical for barangay health workers' effectiveness.

title: Carpenters
rationale: Philippine construction remains highly manual and informal, with site-specific adaptation challenging for AI. Rural and small-scale projects rely heavily on skilled human labor with limited technology infrastructure.

title: Electricians
rationale: Philippine electricians work in highly informal settings with significant on-site physical labor that resists direct automation. Infrastructure limitations and complex local installation contexts further reduce AI replacement potential.

title: Delivery riders
rationale: Philippine delivery riders face high algorithmic management risk through platform apps that can optimize routing and worker allocation. The large informal sector provides some buffer, but platform-driven automation is already transforming the occupation's core tasks.

title: Administrative Services Managers
rationale: AI tools like Microsoft Copilot and advanced workflow management platforms can automate significant administrative coordination tasks. Philippine organizations are increasingly adopting digital transformation strategies that reduce manual administrative coordination.

title: Advertising and Promotions Managers
rationale: AI generative tools are rapidly transforming creative marketing tasks like content generation, campaign design, and audience targeting. Philippine advertising agencies are increasingly adopting digital platforms that enable algorithmic strategy development.

title: Architectural and Engineering Managers
rationale: AI design tools like generative design and parametric modeling are rapidly transforming architectural and engineering workflows. Philippine infrastructure and engineering firms are increasingly adopting digital collaboration platforms that integrate AI-powered design optimization.

title: Biofuels Production Managers
rationale: Philippine biofuels production involves complex technical management where AI can significantly assist in process optimization, predictive maintenance, and performance analytics. Low OFW share and formal sector characteristics make technological integration more feasible.

title: Biomass Power Plant Managers
rationale: Philippine biomass power plants have complex technical operations requiring human oversight, but AI can increasingly assist in predictive maintenance, efficiency optimization, and remote monitoring. Infrastructure limitations and local technical expertise requirements moderate full automation potential.

title: Brownfield Redevelopment Specialists and Site Managers
rationale: Philippine brownfield redevelopment requires complex site-specific assessments with significant local environmental and regulatory nuance that AI cannot easily replicate. Low OFW share and minimal informal employment suggest gradual technological augmentation rather than wholesale replacement.

title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
rationale: Philippine IT managers face high AI disruption through automated project management tools and AI-driven strategic planning systems. The low informal share and high digital literacy make this occupation particularly vulnerable to technological replacement.

title: Farmers, Ranchers, and Other Agricultural Managers
rationale: Philippine agriculture has significant manual labor components that resist full automation, but precision agriculture technologies are increasingly penetrating farm management. Small landholdings and low mechanization rates create both barriers and opportunities for AI-driven agricultural management tools.

title: Food Service Managers
rationale: Philippine food service management faces moderate AI disruption through scheduling, inventory, and customer analytics platforms. Local restaurant ecosystem's informality and personalized service will slow complete automation.

title: Sales Managers
rationale: Sales management in the Philippines faces significant AI disruption through advanced analytics, predictive sales modeling, and automated territory/quota optimization. Large enterprises will likely adopt AI-driven sales management tools faster than smaller local businesses.

title: Security Managers
rationale: Philippine security management involves complex human judgment and contextual understanding that AI currently struggles to replicate. However, AI-powered surveillance, predictive risk analysis, and automated monitoring systems are increasingly sophisticated.

title: Registered Nurses
rationale: AI diagnostic tools and telemedicine are rapidly advancing, potentially replacing routine nursing assessments. However, complex patient care and emotional support remain challenging for AI to fully replicate in the Philippine healthcare context.

title: Software Developers
rationale: AI coding assistants like GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT are already dramatically transforming software development workflows in the Philippines. Large language models can generate code, debug, and even architect complex software systems, reducing manual coding effort.

title: Advanced Practice Psychiatric Nurses
rationale: AI mental health tools are emerging but cannot fully replace human empathy and complex psychological assessment. Philippine healthcare infrastructure and cultural nuances around mental health limit rapid AI substitution.

title: Agricultural Sciences Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Agricultural science teaching in the Philippines involves complex knowledge transfer and research that AI cannot fully replicate. However, AI tools will increasingly assist in curriculum design, research analysis, and lecture preparation.

title: Anthropology and Archeology Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine academic research and teaching remain highly personalized, with significant emphasis on contextual knowledge and local cultural interpretation. AI can assist research and lesson planning, but cannot fully replace the nuanced human expertise required in anthropological scholarship.

title: Area, Ethnic, and Cultural Studies Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Academic research and teaching have nuanced contextual elements that AI struggles to fully replicate, especially in culturally sensitive ethnic studies domains. Philippine universities also maintain strong interpersonal teaching traditions that resist complete technological substitution.

title: Atmospheric, Earth, Marine, and Space Sciences Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine universities have limited research budgets and infrastructure, which slows AI integration in academic sciences. Research-oriented tasks remain complex for current AI, though teaching and administrative work face moderate automation risks.

title: Biological Science Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine postsecondary biology teachers face moderate AI disruption through digital learning platforms and AI-assisted research tools. While complex teaching and research tasks remain human-dependent, lecture content and basic instructional materials can be increasingly AI-generated.

title: Business Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI large language models and generative AI can now produce lecture content, research summaries, and teaching materials with high sophistication. Philippine universities are increasingly adopting digital learning platforms that reduce direct teaching hours.

title: Career/Technical Education Teachers, Middle School
rationale: Philippine technical education relies heavily on human interaction and contextual skill transfer, which AI currently struggles to replicate. Infrastructure limitations and the need for hands-on training reduce immediate automation potential.

title: Career/Technical Education Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Technical education teachers face moderate AI disruption, with AI potentially assisting curriculum design and delivering some instructional content, but human interaction remains crucial for hands-on skills training in Philippine vocational contexts.

title: Career/Technical Education Teachers, Secondary School
rationale: Technical education teachers face moderate AI disruption, with adaptive learning platforms and simulation tools potentially replacing some instructional roles. Philippine technical education's hands-on nature and infrastructure constraints provide some resilience against full automation.

title: Chemistry Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI language models and simulation tools are rapidly advancing in scientific education, potentially automating lecture preparation and basic research tasks. Philippine universities with limited research budgets may be slower to adopt, but digital learning platforms are increasingly competitive.

title: Communications Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI language models can generate lecture content and research drafts, but cannot fully replace nuanced academic communication instruction. Philippine higher education's personalized teaching style and contextual expertise will partially resist complete automation.

title: Computer Network Architects
rationale: AI tools like GitHub Copilot and generative AI are already assisting network design and security configuration. Cloud providers are developing AI-powered network optimization tools that can reduce manual network architecture work.

title: Computer Programmers
rationale: AI coding assistants like GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT are already generating significant code autonomously. Philippine tech outsourcing sector faces high risk of AI-driven productivity substitution.

title: Computer Science Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI tools like ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot are already disrupting computer science curriculum and research workflows. Philippine universities face significant pressure to integrate AI-driven teaching methods and computational tools.

title: Computer Systems Engineers/Architects
rationale: Philippine tech infrastructure is rapidly modernizing, creating opportunities for AI to augment and partially replace systems engineering tasks. Cloud migration and automated infrastructure management are accelerating AI's potential impact.

title: Critical Care Nurses
rationale: Critical care nursing requires complex human judgment, emotional intelligence, and physical patient interaction that AI cannot easily replicate. Philippine healthcare infrastructure and high-touch patient care model further reduce immediate automation risks.

title: Economics Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine economics professors face significant AI disruption through automated lecture preparation, research assistance, and content generation. The high education level and digital literacy of this group makes them more susceptible to AI augmentation and potential partial replacement.

title: Education Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine postsecondary education teachers have complex cognitive tasks that resist full automation, but AI will significantly transform pedagogical methods and research support. Online learning platforms and AI tutoring systems will incrementally reshape instructional delivery.

title: Electronics Engineers, Except Computer
rationale: Electronics engineering faces significant AI disruption through automated design tools, simulation software, and generative AI for circuit design. Low OFW share and formal sector presence accelerate technological transformation.

title: Elementary School Teachers, Except Special Education
rationale: Philippine elementary education relies heavily on personal interaction, emotional intelligence, and culturally nuanced instruction that AI currently cannot replicate. Low OFW share and strong regulatory protection for teaching roles further insulate this occupation from immediate automation risks.

title: Engineering Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Engineering education faces significant AI disruption through automated course content generation, simulation-based learning, and AI tutoring platforms. Philippine universities with limited research infrastructure may be slower to adopt, but global trends will eventually impact local teaching methods.

title: English Language and Literature Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine higher education is experiencing gradual digital transformation, with AI potentially assisting in curriculum design, grading, and research support. However, complex literary analysis and nuanced teaching interactions remain challenging for current AI systems.

title: Environmental Science Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine universities have moderate digital infrastructure for online learning, and AI can assist in research and lecture preparation. However, interactive teaching and field research components remain challenging for full automation.

title: Family and Consumer Sciences Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI can generate course materials and lecture content, but cannot fully replace the interpersonal and pedagogical skills of postsecondary educators. Philippine higher education's emphasis on personal interaction and contextual learning provides some resilience against full automation.

title: Foreign Language and Literature Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI language models and translation tools are rapidly improving, potentially reducing demand for language instruction. However, cultural interpretation and nuanced teaching still require human expertise in the Philippine context.

title: Forestry and Conservation Science Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine academic research and teaching remain highly interpersonal, with complex knowledge transfer requiring nuanced human interaction. AI can assist with research and curriculum development, but cannot fully replace postsecondary educators in forestry and conservation science.

title: History Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine postsecondary history teachers have complex intellectual work that resists full AI replacement, but AI research tools and generative content will significantly transform pedagogical methods. Academic research and nuanced historical interpretation remain challenging for current AI systems.

title: Kindergarten Teachers, Except Special Education
rationale: Philippine kindergarten teaching requires high emotional intelligence and localized cultural adaptation that AI currently cannot replicate. Low OFW share and strong interpersonal requirements protect this occupation from rapid automation.

title: Library Science Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI research tools and generative AI are rapidly transforming academic research and teaching methodologies. Philippine academic institutions are increasingly adopting digital learning platforms, which accelerates potential AI integration in library science instruction.

title: Licensed Practical and Licensed Vocational Nurses
rationale: Philippine healthcare faces significant human touch requirements and infrastructure limitations that slow AI replacement. Nursing requires complex emotional intelligence and physical care tasks that current AI cannot fully replicate.

title: Middle School Teachers, Except Special and Career/Technical Education
rationale: Philippine education system relies heavily on human interaction and contextual understanding that AI currently struggles to replicate. Low OFW share and formal education setting provide some insulation from immediate technological disruption.

title: Nurse Midwives
rationale: High-touch healthcare profession requiring complex human empathy and precise physical interventions that AI cannot easily replicate. Philippine healthcare infrastructure and cultural preferences for personal medical care further reduce automation risk.

title: Nurse Practitioners
rationale: Philippine healthcare relies heavily on personal interaction and contextual understanding, which AI currently struggles to replicate. Low OFW and informal shares suggest direct technological disruption is limited.

title: Nursing Instructors and Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI will significantly impact nursing education through simulation technologies and adaptive learning platforms, but cannot fully replace human instructors' clinical expertise and interpersonal teaching skills. Philippine nursing education's strong emphasis on hands-on clinical training provides a resilient buffer against complete automation.

title: Philosophy and Religion Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine academic philosophy and theology have strong contextual and cultural dimensions that resist pure AI replacement. While research assistance and lecture preparation can be AI-augmented, complex hermeneutic and interpretive tasks remain human-centric.

title: Political Science Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Philippine political science academia faces significant AI disruption through research assistance, automated literature reviews, and AI-generated lecture content. University systems are relatively formal, accelerating potential technology adoption.

title: Postsecondary Teachers, All Other
rationale: Philippine universities are increasingly adopting online learning platforms and AI-assisted teaching tools. Generative AI can already produce lecture materials, draft syllabi, and provide basic instructional content, threatening routine academic preparation tasks.

title: Recreation and Fitness Studies Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: AI can assist with curriculum design and lecture preparation, but cannot fully replace the physical and interpersonal aspects of fitness instruction. Philippine universities have slower technology adoption rates compared to global counterparts.

title: Secondary School Teachers, Except Special and Career/Technical Education
rationale: Philippine secondary teachers face moderate AI disruption, with lesson planning and administrative tasks more automatable than direct classroom instruction. Low OFW share and strong local pedagogical requirements provide some insulation from immediate technological replacement.

title: Advertising Sales Agents
rationale: Philippine advertising sales increasingly rely on digital platforms with algorithmic targeting, reducing human intermediation. Large informal media sector provides some buffer, but programmatic ad buying is rapidly transforming sales workflows.

title: Cashiers
rationale: Philippine retail is rapidly digitalizing with point-of-sale technologies and self-checkout systems. High routine transaction volume makes cashiers prime candidates for AI-driven automation, especially in urban centers with advanced retail infrastructure.

title: Crematory Operators
rationale: Crematory operations require precise physical handling, emotional sensitivity, and complex regulatory compliance that current AI cannot replicate. The low OFW share and moderate informal sector presence further insulate this occupation from rapid automation.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Correctional Officers
rationale: Philippine correctional facilities have significant human interaction requirements and complex interpersonal dynamics that resist full AI automation. Infrastructure limitations and human judgment needs in managing prisoner populations reduce direct technological substitution.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Entertainment and Recreation Workers, Except Gambling Services
rationale: Entertainment supervision involves complex interpersonal interactions that AI struggles to replicate fully. The moderate informal sector share provides some buffer against rapid technological displacement.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Gambling Services Workers
rationale: Casino supervision involves complex human interaction and real-time monitoring that AI currently struggles to replicate fully. The low OFW share and moderate informal economy presence provide some buffer against rapid automation.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Non-Retail Sales Workers
rationale: Sales supervision involves complex interpersonal interactions and strategic decision-making that AI cannot fully replicate. However, AI tools for performance tracking, route optimization, and sales analytics will significantly augment and partially automate supervisory tasks.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Personal Service Workers
rationale: First-line supervisors in personal services face moderate AI disruption, with algorithmic management tools increasingly capable of scheduling, performance tracking, and workflow optimization. Philippine infrastructure and high informal sector share will slow complete automation.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Police and Detectives
rationale: Philippine police supervisory roles involve complex human judgment and situational assessment that AI currently cannot fully replicate. Local infrastructure and cultural context limit rapid technological substitution of law enforcement supervision.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers
rationale: Retail supervisory roles in the Philippines have mixed automation potential due to significant informal sector presence and need for human judgment in personnel management. AI will likely assist in scheduling, performance tracking, and basic analytics, but cannot fully replace interpersonal supervision.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Security Workers
rationale: Philippine security supervision involves significant physical monitoring and human judgment that AI cannot fully replace. However, AI-driven surveillance systems and predictive security analytics are increasingly capable of augmenting traditional supervisory roles.

title: Massage Therapists
rationale: Physical, hands-on therapeutic work requires nuanced human touch and interpersonal assessment that AI cannot easily replicate. High informal sector presence in Philippine massage services further reduces technological substitution risk.

title: Physical Therapist Assistants
rationale: Physical therapy requires nuanced human touch and patient interaction that AI currently struggles to replicate. Philippine healthcare infrastructure and personal care preferences also favor human practitioners.

title: Sales Engineers
rationale: Sales engineering in the Philippines faces significant AI disruption through technical knowledge databases, automated proposal generation, and predictive customer matching. The technical knowledge component makes this role highly susceptible to AI augmentation and partial replacement.

title: Bill and Account Collectors
rationale: AI can automate most communication, payment tracking, and account status workflows through natural language processing and predictive analytics. Low OFW share and moderate formality suggest direct Philippine market disruption.

title: Billing and Posting Clerks
rationale: Billing and posting clerks perform highly routinized, data-entry intensive tasks that are prime candidates for AI and robotic process automation. Philippine digital infrastructure and increasing enterprise software adoption will accelerate this transformation.

title: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks
rationale: AI and machine learning can now automate most routine accounting tasks like data entry, reconciliation, and transaction verification. Philippine accounting firms are rapidly adopting cloud-based accounting software with embedded AI capabilities.

title: Brokerage Clerks
rationale: Brokerage clerks perform highly routine, data-driven tasks that are prime candidates for AI and algorithmic automation. Financial technology platforms are rapidly digitizing and automating securities transaction processing.

title: Correspondence Clerks
rationale: Correspondence clerks perform routine, structured communication tasks highly susceptible to AI language models and template generation. Low OFW share and moderate informal buffer slightly mitigate automation risks.

title: Court, Municipal, and License Clerks
rationale: High potential for AI document processing, form automation, and workflow management in Philippine government offices. Low OFW risk, but significant digital transformation potential in administrative tasks.

title: Credit Authorizers, Checkers, and Clerks
rationale: Credit checking involves routine data processing and pattern recognition, which AI can increasingly handle through machine learning credit scoring models. Philippine banks are rapidly digitalizing credit assessment processes.

title: File Clerks
rationale: Digital document management systems and AI-powered indexing can almost completely replace manual filing tasks in Philippine offices. Cloud storage and intelligent search algorithms make traditional file clerks increasingly obsolete.

title: Financial Clerks, All Other
rationale: Philippine financial clerks face high AI risk due to routine transactional processing that can be easily automated by machine learning and robotic process automation. The low OFW share and relatively formal work environment accelerate potential technological displacement.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers
rationale: AI workflow management tools and generative AI are rapidly replacing traditional supervisory coordination tasks in office environments. Philippine BPO sector's digital maturity accelerates automation potential for administrative supervision.

title: New Accounts Clerks
rationale: Philippine banks are rapidly digitalizing, with AI chatbots and automated onboarding platforms already reducing manual account opening processes. The routine nature of customer intake and documentation makes this role highly susceptible to algorithmic replacement.

title: Office Machine Operators, Except Computer
rationale: Office machine operators handle highly routine, repetitive tasks that are prime candidates for digital and robotic automation. Philippine workplaces are increasingly adopting digital document management systems that can dramatically reduce manual machine operation needs.

title: Hairdressers and beauty workers
rationale: High informal sector presence in Philippine beauty services creates significant barriers to technological replacement. Manual, personalized nature of hairdressing and beauty work requires complex physical and interpersonal skills that current AI cannot easily replicate.

title: Tricycle / jeepney drivers
rationale: High informal sector presence and physical driving requirements create significant barriers to rapid AI automation. Local infrastructure limitations and the need for complex urban navigation in Philippine contexts further reduce immediate displacement risks.

title: Baristas
rationale: Philippine coffee shops have high informal employment and manual preparation techniques that slow automation. However, automated coffee machines and digital ordering platforms are increasingly reducing human interaction.

title: Bartenders
rationale: Philippine bartending remains highly social and personalized, with significant informal sector presence. Local bars and hospitality venues prioritize human interaction and mixology skills that AI cannot easily replicate.

title: Chefs and Head Cooks
rationale: Philippine culinary work remains highly personalized and technique-dependent, with significant informal sector presence. AI can assist with menu planning and inventory, but cannot fully replicate creative cooking skills.

title: Cooks, Fast Food
rationale: Fast food cooking involves repetitive, standardized tasks highly susceptible to robotic and AI-driven automation. The high informal sector share provides some buffer, but large restaurant chains will likely adopt automated cooking systems first.

title: Cooks, Institution and Cafeteria
rationale: Philippine institutional kitchens have significant manual preparation stages and cultural complexity that resist full automation. High informal sector participation (50%) creates friction for rapid technological replacement.

title: Cooks, Private Household
rationale: Philippine household cooking remains highly personalized and culturally nuanced, with significant informal employment. Domestic workers often perform complex culinary tasks requiring cultural knowledge and adaptive skills that current AI cannot replicate.

title: Fast Food and Counter Workers
rationale: Philippine fast food chains are rapidly adopting digital ordering kiosks and mobile app integrations. Large informal sector provides some buffer, but major chains like Jollibee are investing heavily in automation technologies.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Food Preparation and Serving Workers
rationale: Philippine food service supervisors face moderate AI disruption due to emerging restaurant management platforms and scheduling algorithms. The high informal economy share provides some buffer against rapid technological displacement.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Housekeeping and Janitorial Workers
rationale: Philippine hospitality and service sectors have complex human interaction requirements that limit full AI replacement. Informal employment and manual nature of cleaning work create significant automation barriers.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Landscaping, Lawn Service, and Groundskeeping Workers
rationale: Landscaping supervision involves complex coordination and customer interaction that AI cannot fully replace. However, AI-powered route optimization, workforce scheduling, and cost estimation tools will significantly augment and partially automate supervisory tasks.

title: Food Preparation Workers
rationale: Philippine food service has high informal employment, but robotic food preparation and kitchen automation are rapidly advancing. Cloud kitchens and centralized food processing centers will accelerate AI-driven task replacement.

title: Food Preparation and Serving Related Workers, All Other
rationale: Philippine food service workers have significant informal employment and manual tasks that resist full automation. However, kitchen automation technologies and platform-based food delivery are gradually transforming the sector.

title: Food Servers, Nonrestaurant
rationale: Philippine food service workers in non-restaurant settings face moderate AI/automation risk due to emerging robotic food delivery systems and digital order management. High informal sector presence (50%) provides some buffer against rapid technological displacement.

title: Janitors and Cleaners, Except Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
rationale: Physical cleaning tasks require complex spatial navigation and adaptability that current robots struggle with. High informal sector employment further reduces immediate automation risk.

title: Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
rationale: Domestic cleaning requires physical dexterity and contextual adaptation that current AI/robotics struggle to fully replicate in Philippine settings. High informal sector presence and low-cost labor further reduce near-term automation incentives.

title: Waiters and Waitresses
rationale: Philippine restaurants have high informal employment and labor-intensive service models that slow AI replacement. However, digital ordering platforms and robotic serving systems are gradually reducing traditional waiter roles.

title: Fisherfolk
rationale: Philippine fisherfolk operate in highly informal, small-scale settings with limited technological infrastructure. High physical labor component and localized fishing techniques resist direct automation.

title: Agricultural Equipment Operators
rationale: High informal agricultural sector in the Philippines reduces immediate AI automation risk. Limited OFW channel exposure and significant physical task complexity create barriers to rapid technological replacement.

title: Agricultural Inspectors
rationale: High informal agricultural sector in the Philippines creates significant barriers to technological replacement. Physical inspection tasks require nuanced sensory judgment that current AI systems struggle to replicate comprehensively.

title: Agricultural Workers, All Other
rationale: High informal agricultural work in the Philippines creates significant barriers to rapid technological replacement. Rural infrastructure limitations and small landholdings reduce precision agriculture automation potential.

title: Animal Breeders
rationale: High informal agricultural sector provides buffer against rapid AI/automation. Precision breeding technologies exist but require significant local infrastructure and technical expertise.

title: Fallers
rationale: Philippine forestry remains highly manual and informal, with terrain complexity limiting robotic logging. Most fallers work in small-scale, decentralized operations resistant to immediate technological replacement.

title: Farmworkers and Laborers, Crop, Nursery, and Greenhouse
rationale: High informal agricultural sector in the Philippines with low technology penetration creates significant barriers to rapid automation. Physical manual labor tasks remain challenging for current robotic systems, especially in small-scale and diverse crop environments.

title: Farmworkers, Farm, Ranch, and Aquacultural Animals
rationale: High informal agricultural sector in the Philippines provides significant buffer against rapid automation. Physical tasks and local context complexity make full robotic replacement challenging.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Farming, Fishing, and Forestry Workers
rationale: High informal agricultural sector in the Philippines creates significant barriers to rapid technological adoption. Physical supervision and local knowledge remain critical in Philippine farming contexts, limiting AI displacement.

title: Fishing and Hunting Workers
rationale: Philippine fishing and hunting remain highly traditional with low technology penetration. High informal sector participation and localized techniques create significant barriers to rapid AI/automation replacement.

title: Forest and Conservation Workers
rationale: Philippine forest conservation work remains highly manual and location-specific, with significant physical tasks requiring direct human judgment in challenging terrain. High informal sector participation (85%) further insulates these workers from rapid technological displacement.

title: Graders and Sorters, Agricultural Products
rationale: High informal agricultural sector provides some buffer against rapid AI adoption, but computer vision and sorting technologies are rapidly advancing for agricultural product grading. Philippine agricultural infrastructure limitations partially slow automation.

title: Log Graders and Scalers
rationale: High informal sector presence in Philippine logging reduces immediate AI automation risk. Physical inspection and manual measurement tasks still require human judgment and tactile skills.

title: Construction Laborers
rationale: Philippine construction remains highly labor-intensive with significant informal workforce. Infrastructure limitations and manual complexity reduce immediate AI/robotic substitution potential.

title: Computer, Automated Teller, and Office Machine Repairers
rationale: Philippine repair technicians face significant AI and automation risks due to increasing self-diagnostic capabilities and modular hardware replacement strategies. The high informal sector share provides some buffer, but technological standardization will accelerate replacement.

title: Control and Valve Installers and Repairers, Except Mechanical Door
rationale: Philippine infrastructure's mixed technological adoption creates moderate AI disruption risk. Physical installation tasks resist full automation, but diagnostic and maintenance processes can be significantly augmented by AI-driven predictive maintenance systems.

title: Helpers--Carpenters
rationale: Construction helper roles in the Philippines have significant physical tasks and high informal sector participation, which slows direct automation. However, emerging construction technologies and modular building techniques could gradually reduce manual helper demand.

title: Helpers--Electricians
rationale: Philippine construction's high informal sector and manual nature create significant barriers to rapid electrician helper automation. Low-skill physical tasks require complex robotic manipulation that remains challenging in PH infrastructure contexts.

title: Helpers--Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Workers
rationale: High informal sector presence reduces immediate automation risk. Physical assistance tasks require contextual understanding that current AI struggles to replicate in Philippine work environments.

title: Helpers--Pipelayers, Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
rationale: High informal sector presence and physical nature of work create significant barriers to automation. Philippine construction infrastructure and manual labor contexts resist rapid technological displacement.

title: Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Workers, All Other
rationale: Philippine infrastructure limitations and high informal sector share slow direct automation, but emerging robotic and AI-assisted maintenance technologies will gradually transform repair workflows. Specialized repair tasks remain challenging for AI, creating a mixed automation landscape.

title: Maintenance and Repair Workers, General
rationale: High informal sector share and physical complexity of repair tasks create significant barriers to full automation. Philippine infrastructure limitations and need for adaptive problem-solving reduce immediate AI replacement risk.

title: Mechanical Door Repairers
rationale: Philippine construction trades have significant informal work practices that slow technology adoption. Mechanical door repair requires physical skills and on-site troubleshooting that current AI cannot easily replicate.

title: Operating Engineers and Other Construction Equipment Operators
rationale: Construction equipment is increasingly automated with GPS-guided machinery and semi-autonomous operation. Philippine infrastructure projects are adopting precision technologies, but high informal sector and manual intervention still provide some buffer.

title: Pile Driver Operators
rationale: Pile driving requires complex physical coordination and site-specific spatial reasoning that current AI and robotic systems struggle to replicate in Philippine construction environments. High informal sector participation further reduces immediate automation risk.

title: Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
rationale: High informal sector presence and physical complexity of plumbing work create significant barriers to AI/robotic automation in the Philippine context. Manual skills, spatial reasoning, and on-site problem-solving remain challenging for current AI technologies.

title: Rail-Track Laying and Maintenance Equipment Operators
rationale: Philippine rail infrastructure remains relatively manual and labor-intensive, with significant informal employment buffering technological disruption. Specialized equipment requires skilled human operation and maintenance in challenging terrain.

title: Seafarers / merchant marine
rationale: Maritime automation through autonomous ships and digital navigation systems poses significant disruption risk for Filipino seafarers, particularly through international shipping channels. High OFW share (0.15) means technological displacement can occur rapidly in destination maritime markets.

title: Airline Pilots, Copilots, and Flight Engineers
rationale: Philippine airline pilots face significant AI automation risk through advanced autopilot systems and autonomous flight technologies. International aviation standards and global technological convergence accelerate automation potential despite local infrastructure limitations.

title: Bus Drivers, Transit and Intercity
rationale: Autonomous vehicle technology is rapidly advancing, with significant potential to displace bus drivers in urban and intercity routes. Philippine infrastructure challenges slow full replacement, but algorithmic route optimization and autonomous transit pilots are already emerging.

title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Operators
rationale: CNC tool operators face high automation risk due to direct programmability and predictable physical tasks. Philippine manufacturing's gradual technological upgrading increases AI/robotics replacement potential.

title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Programmers
rationale: CNC programming is highly susceptible to AI-driven code generation and optimization algorithms. Philippine manufacturing's gradual technological upgrading increases AI integration potential, especially in export-oriented sectors.

title: Driver/Sales Workers
rationale: Philippine delivery and transport workers face high algorithmic management risk through platform-based routing and autonomous vehicle technologies. The significant informal sector provides some buffer, but gig economy platforms are rapidly transforming logistics work.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Helpers, Laborers, and Material Movers, Hand
rationale: Philippine manufacturing supervisors face moderate AI risk due to emerging logistics optimization and workforce management algorithms. Infrastructure limitations and high informal sector presence will slow complete automation.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Material-Moving Machine and Vehicle Operators
rationale: Philippine logistics and transport supervision faces significant AI disruption through route optimization, predictive maintenance algorithms, and autonomous vehicle management technologies. The moderate informal sector buffer slows but does not prevent technological displacement.
